texto: Government — or an administration — isn’t really the sum of its parts like people think, because all of those parts are competing for their own interests and pulling in opposite directions at the same time. It’s like an organism at war with itself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Craig A. Falconer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Government — or an administration — isn’t really the sum of its parts like people think, because …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, government, parts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
